context_of_use: Deterministic synthetic engineering QA for ECG R-peak detection and observable R-R interval reconstruction under controlled acquisition disturbances.

acceptance_profile.description: Pre-specified acceptance limits for the complete R-peak and RR noise protocol.
RR aggregate MAE for all cases except the isolated -12 dB external-noise stress case.
RR aggregate MAE for the isolated -12 dB external-noise stress case.

truth_policy.event_scoreability: ECG R-peak truth requires complete in-record QRS support and more than 5% retained signal during all-lead dropout; every exclusion and nearby ignored prediction is explicitly reported.
truth_policy.rr_definition: Difference between consecutive present-QRS construction R-peak times inside the record; an interval touching unscoreable R-peak truth is not_evaluable.
truth_policy.first_beat: Excluded because no preceding in-record R peak exists for RR construction.
truth_policy.artifact_overlap: Additive and external noise, clipping, saturation and partial-lead artifacts remain scoreable. The -12 dB case remains severe stress and uses its pre-specified RR acceptance stratum.
truth_policy.boundary: Only QRS truth whose constructed support crosses the record boundary is excluded; there is no arbitrary blanket last-beat window.

evidence_boundary: Synthetic engineering evidence only; not FDA qualification, clinical validation, diagnostic performance evidence, or a substitute for sponsor-specific risk analysis and representative clinical data.
